lineage: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
Athanasius, passing through Jerusalem on his Return to Alexandria, is received into Communion by Maximus: and a Synod of Bishops, convened in that City, confirms the Nicene Creed.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: Athanasius, passing through Jerusalem on his Return to Alexandria, is received into Communion by Maximus: and a Synod of Bishops, convened in that City, confirms the Nicene Creed.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Athanasius the bishop being fortified with such letters as these, passed through Syria, and came into Palestine.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: On arriving at Jerusalem he acquainted Maximus the bishop both with what had been done in the Council of Sardica, and also that the Emperor Constantius had confirmed its decision: he then proposed that a Synod of the bishops there should be held.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Maximus, therefore, without delay sent for certain of the bishops of Syria and Palestine, and having assembled a council, he restored Athanasius to communion, and to his former dignity.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: After which the Synod communicated by letter to the Alexandrians, and to all the bishops of Egypt and Libya, what had been determined respecting Athanasius.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Whereupon the adversaries of Athanasius exceedingly derided Maximus, because having before assisted in his deposition, he had suddenly changed his mind, and as if nothing had previously taken place, had voted for his restoration to communion and rank.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: When Ursacius and Valens, who had been fiery partisans of Arianism, ascertained these things, condemning their former zeal, they proceeded to Rome, where they presented their recantation to Julius the bishop, and gave their assent to the doctrine of consubstantiality: they also wrote to Athanasius, and expressed their readiness to hold communion with him in future.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Thus Ursacius and Valens were at that time subdued by the good fortune of Athanasius and induced to recognize the orthodox faith.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Athanasius passed through Pelusium on his way to Alexandria, and admonished the inhabitants of every city to beware of the Arians, and to receive those only that professed the Homoousian faith.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: In some of the churches also he performed ordination; which afforded another ground of accusation against him, because of his undertaking to ordain in the dioceses of others.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Such was the progress of affairs at that period in reference to Athanasius.
